data.title: Radiance Holdings’ Key Subsidiary Swings to Q1 Loss on Lower Revenue
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

The latest announcement is out from Radiance Holdings (Group) Company Limited ( (HK:9993) ).

Radiance Holdings has disclosed a summary of the unaudited first-quarter 2026 financial results of its indirect non-wholly owned subsidiary, Radiance Group Co., Ltd., as required by PRC rules tied to the unit’s outstanding debt instruments. The property-focused subsidiary reported total assets of RMB76.681 billion and total liabilities of RMB60.398 billion as of 31 March 2026, with equity attributable to owners slightly down to RMB16.283 billion and cash and cash equivalents essentially stable at RMB827 million.

For the three months ended 31 March 2026, Radiance Group’s operating revenue fell to RMB1.643 billion from RMB2.417 billion a year earlier, while gross profit margin narrowed to 13.50%, resulting in an operating loss of RMB23 million and a net loss of RMB19 million, compared with a profit in the prior-year period. The company highlighted that these unaudited figures, prepared under PRC accounting standards, are not directly comparable with Radiance Holdings’ Hong Kong reporting and do not provide a full picture of the group’s consolidated operating or financial condition, a caveat relevant for investors assessing credit and equity risk tied to the subsidiary’s debt.

More about Radiance Holdings (Group) Company Limited

Radiance Holdings (Group) Company Limited is a Cayman Islands–incorporated investment holding company listed in Hong Kong that operates through subsidiaries in China’s property and related sectors. Its indirect non-wholly owned unit Radiance Group Co., Ltd. issues onshore debt financing instruments in the PRC and reports quarterly financials under China Accounting Standards for Business Enterprises.

Average Trading Volume: 9,328,333

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$4.25B

data.title: Hang Lung Group Shareholders Approve All AGM Resolutions, Back Capital Mandates
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Hang Lung Group ( (HK:0010) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Hang Lung Group reported that all resolutions at its April 30, 2026 annual general meeting were approved by shareholders via poll, including the adoption of the 2025 audited financial statements, declaration of a final dividend, and the re-election of key board members. Shareholders also backed the reappointment of KPMG as auditor and granted the board general mandates to repurchase and issue shares, signaling continued support for the company’s capital management flexibility and existing governance structure.

The resolution to authorize the board to issue additional shares and the related extension mandate passed with notably lower, though still majority, support than other items, indicating some shareholder caution around potential equity dilution. With broad participation, no voting restrictions, and an independent scrutineer overseeing the poll, the AGM outcomes reinforce management’s mandate while highlighting areas of investor sensitivity around share issuance policies.

More about Hang Lung Group

Hang Lung Group Limited is a Hong Kong-incorporated investment holding company with interests primarily in property development and investment, operating within the real estate sector. The group focuses on owning, developing, and managing commercial and residential properties in Hong Kong and mainland China, positioning itself as a long-term player in Greater China’s property market.

The latest announcement is out from CITIC ( (HK:0267) ).

CITIC Limited has announced that its wholly owned subsidiary, CITIC Corporation Limited, has released its financial statements and auditor’s report for the year ended 31 December 2025, prepared under PRC Generally Accepted Accounting Principles. The disclosures, required because CITIC Corporation is an issuer of bonds and short-term commercial paper in China, are now publicly available via the Shanghai Stock Exchange and the National Association of Financial Market Institutional Investors, enhancing transparency for mainland debt investors and other stakeholders.

The publication underscores CITIC Corporation’s compliance with regulatory reporting obligations imposed by the People’s Bank of China, the China Securities Regulatory Commission and other market bodies. While the announcement itself provides no headline financial figures, it signals ongoing adherence to disclosure standards that can influence investor confidence and supports the company’s standing in China’s onshore capital markets.

CITIC Corporation operates in China’s financial markets and has issued corporate bonds and super and short-term commercial paper in the domestic market, bringing it under the oversight of key mainland regulators and stock exchanges.

data.title: China Power’s SPIC Hydropower Q1 Net Profit Jumps Despite Revenue Decline
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

An update from China Power International Development ( (HK:2380) ) is now available.

China Power International Development reported that its 55.13%-owned subsidiary SPIC Hydropower posted an 18.55% year-on-year decline in first-quarter 2026 revenue to RMB2.47 billion, but net profit attributable to shareholders surged 168% to RMB393 million. Basic earnings per share tripled to RMB0.09, supported by higher underlying profitability, as net profit excluding extraordinary items rose 176.2%, while total assets and shareholder equity grew modestly, though the company cautioned that these figures reflect only the subsidiary and not the group’s overall financial position.

SPIC Hydropower’s comparative figures for early 2025 were restated under PRC GAAP rules for business combinations under common control following an asset restructuring that made it a subsidiary of China Power. The restructuring has allowed SPIC Hydropower to account for acquired operations as if owned in prior periods, clarifying performance trends for investors, but China Power advised shareholders and potential investors not to rely solely on the subsidiary’s results when assessing the group as a whole.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2380) stock is a Hold with a HK$3.30 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on China Power International Development stock, see the HK:2380 Stock Forecast page.

More about China Power International Development

China Power International Development is a Hong Kong–incorporated power producer with a growing focus on hydropower through its majority-owned subsidiary SPIC Hydropower Co., Ltd., which is listed on the Shanghai Stock Exchange. The group operates within China’s electricity sector, expanding its renewable generation portfolio and consolidating assets under common control to strengthen its position in low‑carbon power.

China Jicheng Holdings Limited ( (HK:1027) ) has issued an announcement.

Asia Strategy Digit Technology Holdings Limited has called its annual general meeting for 1 June 2026 in Hong Kong to present audited consolidated financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and to seek shareholder approval on a series of routine corporate matters. The agenda focuses on corporate governance continuity, with resolutions to re-elect two executive directors and one independent non-executive director, authorise the board to set directors’ pay, and re-appoint Infinity CPA Limited as auditor with board-approved remuneration.

Shareholders will also vote on a general mandate empowering the board to issue, allot and deal in new shares up to 20% of the company’s issued share capital, excluding rights issues, option exercises, scrip dividends and conversions of existing instruments. If approved, this mandate would give the board added flexibility to raise capital or pursue strategic transactions within prescribed limits, potentially influencing future financing activities and the company’s capital structure.

The latest update is out from Sunshine Oilsands ( (HK:2012) ).

Sunshine Oilsands has again postponed the mailing of a shareholder circular related to a proposed placing of up to 114,280,000 new shares under a specific mandate, pushing the expected despatch date from April 30 to on or before May 29, 2026. The delay, attributed to additional time needed to prepare and incorporate required information, adds further uncertainty around the timing of the share issuance and its completion, prompting the company to urge shareholders and potential investors to exercise caution when trading its Hong Kong-listed shares.

More about Sunshine Oilsands

The latest update is out from Xinyi Glass Holdings ( (HK:0868) ).

Xinyi Glass Holdings has called its annual general meeting for 29 May 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will review the audited financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and vote on a proposed final dividend of HK$0.215 per share. The meeting will also consider the re-election of several executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, re-appointment of Ernst & Young as auditor, and authorisations for the board to set directors’ and auditor’s remuneration.

Shareholders will be asked to renew a mandate allowing the board to repurchase up to 10% of the company’s issued share capital, excluding treasury shares, over the coming year, subject to regulatory limits. They will also vote on a general mandate enabling the board to issue new shares or deal in treasury shares, providing management with capital management flexibility that could influence future funding, share capital structure and shareholder returns.

More about Xinyi Glass Holdings

Xinyi Glass Holdings is a Hong Kong-listed glass manufacturer, primarily engaged in producing and selling float glass, automobile glass and architectural glass for construction and automotive markets. The group serves both domestic and international customers, positioning itself as a major supplier within the broader building materials and auto supply chains in Greater China and beyond.

An announcement from Yankuang Energy Group Company Limited Class H ( (HK:1171) ) is now available.

Yankuang Energy Group has completed the issuance of its 2026 first tranche of sci-tech innovation bonds designated for M&A activities, raising RMB 2 billion in China’s interbank bond market. The medium-term notes, carrying a 1.84% interest rate and a 3+N year term, were fully subscribed and funds were received on 29 April 2026, reflecting the company’s continued access to low-cost domestic funding channels and its intent to support merger-and-acquisition-driven growth.

China Merchants Bank acted as bookrunner and lead underwriter, with Industrial Bank and Agricultural Bank of China as joint-lead underwriters, underscoring strong backing from major Chinese financial institutions. The successful placement of these sci-tech innovation bonds is likely to strengthen Yankuang Energy’s balance sheet flexibility for future transactions and signals confidence from institutional investors in its credit profile and expansion strategy.

An update from Beijing Enterprises Urban Resources Group Ltd. ( (HK:3718) ) is now available.

Beijing Enterprises Urban Resources Group Limited announced that its 85%-owned Hong Kong subsidiary, Shiny Glory Services Limited, has won a major tender from the Leisure and Cultural Services Department to provide cleaning and supporting services to leisure venues across several Hong Kong districts. The three-year contracts, expected to commence on 1 May 2026, carry an estimated total value of HK$964.54 million and annual revenue of about HK$321.51 million, although final figures may vary as formal contracts have not yet been signed.

The successful bid reinforces the group’s position in Hong Kong’s environmental hygiene market and signals a meaningful expansion of its services portfolio and revenue base, while the company cautions investors that actual contract value and income could change with market conditions.

data.title: Easou Technology Sets 2026 AGM to Renew Mandates and Reappoint Auditor
data.description:

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Easou Technology Holdings Limited ( (HK:2550) ) has issued an update.

Easou Technology Holdings Limited has convened its annual general meeting for June 30, 2026, in Shenzhen, where shareholders will review and adopt the audited consolidated financial statements and directors’ and auditor’s reports for the year ended December 31, 2025. The agenda also includes re-election of executive directors Wang Xi and Chen Jun, authorization for the board to set directors’ and auditor’s remuneration, re-appointment of Ernst & Young as auditor for 2026, and renewal of a general mandate allowing the board to issue additional shares within prescribed limits.

These proposed resolutions, if approved, will maintain continuity in Easou’s senior leadership and external auditing arrangements while preserving board flexibility to raise capital through share issuance. The general mandate to allot and deal with additional shares, including treasury shares and convertible securities, underscores the company’s intent to keep financing and capital-structure options open, which may influence future dilution levels and funding strategies for its technology-focused operations.

More about Easou Technology Holdings Limited

Easou Technology Holdings Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange under stock code 2550. The company operates in the technology sector, although the specific products or services are not detailed in the notice, and it maintains operational presence in Shenzhen, China, where its annual general meeting will be held.

An update from Guanze Medical Information Industry (Holding) Co., Ltd. ( (HK:2427) ) is now available.

Guanze Medical Information Industry (Holding) Co., Ltd. has called its annual general meeting for 29 May 2026 in Hong Kong, where shareholders will review the audited financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and vote on the re-election of two independent non-executive directors, along with authorising the board to set directors’ pay. Investors will also decide on the re-appointment of Moore CPA Limited as auditor and on granting the board a general mandate to issue up to 20% of the company’s share capital, a move that could provide funding flexibility for future corporate or strategic initiatives while potentially diluting existing holdings.

More about Guanze Medical Information Industry (Holding) Co., Ltd.

Guanze Medical Information Industry (Holding) Co., Ltd. is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong that operates in the medical information industry. It focuses on providing medical information-related products and services, positioning itself within the broader healthcare and technology market in the region.
